title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Swollen Gums
intro: Herbal teas for swollen gums are a natural remedy that involves drinking specific teas made from herbs to alleviate inflammation and discomfort in the gums. These teas can treat swollen gums by reducing inflammation, fighting off bacteria, and promoting healing, due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Examples of herbal teas that can help with swollen gums include peppermint tea, which reduces inflammation and eases pain, chamomile tea, which soothes and calms the gums, ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties, eucalyptus tea, which reduces swelling and fights off bacteria, sage tea, which has antibacterial properties, and licorice root tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes healing. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can find relief from swollen gums and promote overall oral health.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums, a common symptom of various dental and gum infections. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and combat bacterial growth in the gums. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, in Echinacea purpurea teas may contribute to its ability to treat swollen gums by modulating the immune response and reducing oxidative stress. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea regularly may help to alleviate swollen gums by promoting healing, reducing pain, and preventing further infection, providing relief for those suffering from this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the flowers and leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea purpurea in the tea infuser or cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea and let it cool before drinking.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat swollen gums, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat swollen gums, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to be cautious when combining with other medications or herbal preparations, as it may interact with them.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of tea 2-3 times a day for swollen gums.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea purpurea per 1 cup of boiling water.
Continue tea consumption for 5-7 days to alleviate swollen gums.
Consult a doctor if swollen gums persist after 7 days of tea use.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums, an ailment often caused by gum inflammation. The antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce gum swelling and prevent further infection. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects, exerting their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to soothe and protect the gums. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat swollen gums include reduced inflammation, accelerated healing, and a lowered risk of further infections, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common oral health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe swollen gums.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology", Salvia officinalis teas for swollen gums have been shown to be effective in alleviating symptoms of oral mucositis and gingivitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat swollen gums, but consuming them may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, take precautions like starting with small doses, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding excessive consumption to prevent interactions with other medications or exacerbating the condition.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea 3 times a day
Steep tea for 5-7 minutes before drinking
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Salvia officinalis per cup of tea
Drink tea after meals and before bedtime for better results
Continue usage for 7-10 days to see significant improvement

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums, a common condition characterized by inflammation and pain. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of ginger tea help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, making it an effective herbal remedy for this ailment. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat swollen gums include reduced inflammation, pain relief, and improved oral health, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and place it in a cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce swollen gums.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat swollen gums, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, take it in moderation, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and limit the frequency of consumption to 1-2 times a day.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids. Let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Gently rinse your gums with the cooled tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 cup of tea for 2-3 days to see noticeable improvements in swollen gums.
Repeat the process as needed, ideally in the morning and evening for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used for centuries to treat swollen gums, a common condition characterized by inflammation and pain in the gums. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation, attributed to its bioactive constituents such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas provide relief from the discomfort associated with swollen gums, promoting a faster recovery and improved oral health. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat swollen gums include reduced swelling, alleviated pain, and improved overall oral health, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Glycyrrhiza glabra roots or 2 teaspoons of dried roots.
Cut the fresh roots into small pieces or crush the dried roots.
Combine the roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe swollen gums.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Indian journal of dental research : official publication of Indian Society for Dental Research", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may be beneficial in treating swollen gums due to its potential antibacterial activity against primary plaque colonizers and periodontal pathogens.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat swollen gums, but it may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, headaches, and potassium deficiency due to its licorice root content. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is essential to limit the consumption to short periods (no longer than 2-3 weeks) and monitor your blood pressure and potassium levels, as well as avoid taking it with other potassium-depleting medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of dried root powder with each tea.
Steep the root powder in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink the tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable results.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist after 10 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums, a common symptom of various oral health issues. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that cause gum swelling. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus, including eucalyptol, cineole, and flavonoids, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas has been shown to provide relief from swollen gums, promote oral health, and prevent the progression of gum diseases.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the leaves in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce swollen gums.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat swollen gums, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to eucalyptus. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it to avoid over-stimulation of the gums, and to avoid consuming it before bedtime to minimize potential disruptions to sleep.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea for 5-7 minutes.
Strain tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Soak a cotton swab in cooled tea for 1 minute.
Gently apply the swab to swollen gums for 5 minutes, 3 times a day.
Use Eucalyptus globulus tea 2-3 times a week for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums, also known as gingivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, which contributes to the swollen gums. Bioactive constituents such as rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and apigenin in Melissa officinalis teas have been shown to exhibit these properties, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat swollen gums include reducing pain and discomfort, promoting healing, and providing a natural alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of Melissa officinalis dried flowers to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain.
Drink the tea as needed, up to 3 times a day.
Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat swollen gums ailment, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include avoiding it if you are pregnant or breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions and affect milk production, and also being cautious when combining it with other medications.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Melissa officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and let it cool to room temperature.
Use 1-2 cups of the cooled tea as a mouthwash 2-3 times a day.
Swish the tea around your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out.
Continue using the tea for 7-10 days to reduce swollen gum inflammation.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums, thereby alleviating the swelling. Drinking yarrow tea can help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. The benefits of using yarrow tea to treat swollen gums include its natural and non-invasive approach, making it a popular alternative remedy for this common ailment.

Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the gathered leaves and flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste, if desired. The recommended dosage is 1/2 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 2 days. Reheat it before consuming.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat swollen gums, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When using Achillea millefolium teas to treat swollen gums, precautions include being cautious of potential interactions with blood thinners and other medications, as well as pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies to plants in the same family.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Achillea millefolium tea 2 times a day.
Steep tea for 5-7 minutes before consumption.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried herb for 1 cup of boiling water.
Consume tea for 5-7 days to see noticeable relief.
Monitor gum condition and adjust tea frequency as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as wild marjoram, have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Origanum vulgare, including carvacrol and thymol, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that cause gum swelling. By consuming Origanum vulgare teas, individuals can benefit from the antiseptic and analgesic effects, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. Regular consumption of these teas can also promote oral health and prevent further complications, making them a beneficial herbal remedy for treating swollen gums.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Heat the water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the dried Origanum vulgare leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Environmental science and pollution research international, Origanum vulgare teas were cited among the herbal remedies used in the north-central region of Morocco for treating oral and gum infections, including swollen gums.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat swollen gums, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to the high concentration of phenolic compounds. When using Origanum vulgare teas, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it to minimize interactions with other medications and potential allergic reactions, and to avoid taking it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Syzygium aromaticum teas, also known as clove teas, have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents, including eugenol, caryophyllene, and beta-caryophyllene, present in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums. By consuming Syzygium aromaticum teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to numb the affected area, reduce swelling, and promote healing, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with swollen gums. Regular consumption of Syzygium aromaticum teas may also help to prevent the progression of gum disease and promote overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Syzygium aromaticum (Clove buds) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Syzygium aromaticum in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce swelling in the gums.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Natural products and bioprospecting, Syzygium aromaticum teas were the most commonly prescribed remedy by local herbalists in Casablanca, Morocco for swollen gums, with a high use value of 0.94.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Syzygium aromaticum teas can be used to treat swollen gums, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with blood thinners, which can lead to bleeding or bruising. To use these teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid taking them for extended periods, and be cautious when consuming them with other medications, especially blood thinners.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat swollen gums, also known as gingivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides and glycosides, help to reduce inflammation and combat the bacterial infections that cause swollen gums. By consuming Echinacea angustifolia teas, individuals can help to alleviate symptoms of gingivitis, including redness and swelling, and promote overall oral health. The benefits of using Echinacea angustifolia teas for swollen gums include reduced pain and discomfort, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function to prevent future infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Echinacea roots in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Echinacea roots.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea using a tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1 cup of the Echinacea tea 2-3 times a day to help with swollen gums.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be beneficial in treating swollen gums, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interact with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. To minimize the risk, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid taking it for extended periods, and be cautious when consuming it if you have any underlying health conditions, such as kidney or liver disease.

preparation_study: According to Dentistry journal, Echinacea purpurea teas for swollen gums may provide anti-inflammatory actions that can help alleviate swollen gums, as part of a toothpaste containing herbal extracts showed anti-inflammatory effects in a clinical study on patients with gingivitis and periodontitis.
intro_study: According to the study, sage tea, in combination with thyme and peppermint, has been shown to effectively alleviate oral mucositis, which may help in reducing swollen gums.
